task: Daily scout performance analysis — 2026-04-17 (scout→snipe conversion failure)
local_output: 6 trades 4W/2L net -0.6p; 72 scout findings but 0 became snipes/trades
opus_correction: Root cause: scout→snipe promoter fully disconnected today — 72 findings × 15 pairs × sniper_score up to 80 yielded 0 snipe_created/0 snipe_triggered. All 6 fills came through snipe_direct (5) or direct scout (1). Most plausible trigger: tuning override #178 (snipe.direction_source rewire) from today's 18-override guardian redesign. BB-threshold propagation bug confirmed a 3rd time: watch #1934 AUD_USD has bb_bandwidth:0.0035 in trigger_conditions but not in the gradable conditions JSON. BB gate threshold is 3.0p in code vs 6.0p in task file (stale).
opus_reasoning: Followed the full funnel: scout_findings → watch_manifest → snipe_created → snipe_triggered → live_trades. Every stage except the first collapsed to zero — meaning the failure is in the promoter, not the scout engine. Cross-referenced with today's 18 tuning_overrides table entries: 17 of them were post-market-close. Only the direction_source rewire could have affected mid-day trading because it's read each cycle. BB manifest→conditions copier bug is pre-existing (14-day history shows 49/72 watches use the boolean flag instead of the numeric threshold). Recommendations grouped by impact: fix promoter (blocks all scout value), wire gates into snipe_direct (plug cooldown/daily_limit/fan_exhaustion leaks), fix BB copier (increases gradable watch quality).

task: Connection Doctor repair — on_demand API false-positive root cause + FUSE/SQLite lockup
local_output: 85-item repair queue backlog; DB writes fail with SQLITE_IOERR from sandbox
opus_correction: Two independent root causes. (1) API-sentry false positives: cycle.py::_run_api_sentry was not consulting expected_state like _run_db_sentry and _run_mcp_sentry do, so every idle cycle of on_demand MLX/ollama servers re-raised API_ERROR even after incident_commander resolved it → 80 stale queue entries. Patch: api_sentry now skips expected_state.mode in ('on_demand','disabled'). (2) Sandbox write failures: connection_doctor.db uses journal_mode=DELETE and the live serve_ui on the Mac holds an exclusive rollback-journal lock during its sentry cycles. The Cowork FUSE bind-mount surfaces this as SQLITE_IOERR rather than SQLITE_BUSY, so busy_timeout retries don't help. Workaround: PRAGMA journal_mode=MEMORY + synchronous=OFF for sandbox-side writes; permanent fix: restart serve_ui so the patched cycle.py stops raising these alerts at the source.
opus_reasoning: The on_demand design is correct (servers spin up only when needed) but the sentry check predates it — classic drift between infra contract and monitor logic. Fixing the monitor (not the servers) is the right direction. For the SQLite lock issue, the fact that Python/Markdown writes in the same directory tree work fine is the tell: it's journaling-mode interaction with the FUSE bind, not a general permissions problem. Preventive fix of adding mlx_cro to expected_state stops a future recurrence of the same class of alert. Handoff pattern: commit what you can (code changes, markdown reports, cleanup scripts) and leave DB state fixes to a host-side script — produces an idempotent two-part repair that doesn't need to be re-run if the host work lags.
